shortAnswer: Anti-HCV antibodies indicate that the body has had contact with the hepatitis C virus at some point. A positive test doesn't automatically mean an active infection, it must be confirmed by an additional test for the virus itself (PCR).
metaDescription: Anti-HCV positive? Meaning for a hepatitis C infection explained simply.
doctorQuestions: Is an additional PCR test needed for confirmation?
Do I have an active or resolved infection?
